zh-hans.Nottinghamshire, England: 英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe于1391年出生。她和Sir William De Thorpe至少有1个儿子。她去世于1350年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe was born in 1391. She had at least 1 son with Sir William De Thorpe. She died in 1350, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe est née en 1391. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir William De Thorpe. Elle est décédée en 1350, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe wurde 1391 geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir William De Thorpe mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1350 in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe è nata il nel 1391. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir William De Thorpe. Morì nel 1350, nel Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Nottinghamshire, England: イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpeは1391年に生まれた。彼女はSir William De Thorpeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1350年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe(은)는 1391년 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir William De Thorpe(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe nasceu em 1391. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir William De Thorpe. Ela faleceu em 1350, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe родилась в 1391 г. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir William De Thorpe. Она умерла в 1350 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret De Thorpe nació en 1391. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir William De Thorpe. Murió en 1350, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
